cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out which question hasn't been discussed in the passage. The question is asking which of the options (A to D) hasn't been covered in the text. First, I'll go through each option and check if the passage addresses it. Option A: "Why are men smarter than women?" The passage mentions that men are more represented in the top 2% and that they believe they are smarter. It also discusses that male developmental programs might be tilted towards high achievement. So there's some explanation about why men might be considered smarter. Option B: "Why are men more stupid than women?" The passage says men are not only more in the top 2% but also the bottom 2%. It suggests that men might be more variable in their intelligence, possibly due to societal expectations and risk-taking. So the passage does address why men are more in the lower percentiles, which relates to being "more stupid." Option C: "How does the result go along with the past research?" The passage mentions the research aligns with past findings, like men getting more firsts and thirds, women getting seconds. It also references a past study on coursework favoring women's steady approach. So this is discussed. Option D: "How can we help the men in the bottom?"
